articles.title: Sony Pictures Makes $100M Strategic Investment in Immersive LBE Company Cosm
articles.summary: Sony Pictures Entertainment is acquiring a minority stake in Cosm, a location-based entertainment technology company that builds immersive, dome-based experiences. The $100 million infusion will fund Cosm's expansion of LBE venues and enhance its technology platform across sports and entertainment. This signals growing major-media appetite for physical immersive entertainment, a space FEC operators should watch closely as competition for out-of-home entertainment dollars intensifies.

articles.title: DIVR Labs to Debut New VR Game 'Castle Crusade' for Bowling Centers at Bowl Expo 2026
articles.summary: DIVR Labs will showcase Castle Crusade, a new multiplayer free-roam VR title for its self-service InstaVR platform, at Bowl Expo 2026 in Nashville (June 28–July 2). The InstaVR system requires no dedicated staff, making it a low-labor upsell option for bowling centers and other FECs. Operators attending Bowl Expo can demo the attraction at Booth #875 alongside the full InstaVR Arena lineup.

articles.title: AAMA Executive Vice President Pete Gustafson Resigns After 10 Years
articles.summary: The American Amusement Machine Association announced that EVP Pete Gustafson has resigned effective June 28, 2026, following a decade of leadership. Gustafson has been a central figure in coin-op and amusement machine industry advocacy, making this a notable leadership transition for the arcade and redemption sector. FEC operators and suppliers who engage with AAMA should watch for updates on interim leadership and any shifts in association direction.

articles.title: Semnox Deploys Parafait Cashless Platform at New Multi-Attraction FEC in Maryland
articles.summary: K&K Adventure Zone in Prince Frederick, Maryland — featuring arcade, laser tag, VR, mini-golf, and a laser maze — partnered with Semnox Solutions and Betson Enterprises to implement the Parafait cashless platform including LuminOS game readers, self-service kiosks, and a centralized POS. The deployment illustrates how mid-size FECs are using integrated technology stacks to streamline multi-attraction operations with minimal staff friction. This is a practical case study for operators evaluating cashless and self-service upgrades.

articles.title: Hasbro-Branded Family Entertainment Center Coming to Pigeon Forge in Summer 2027
articles.summary: A Hasbro-branded FEC is set to open in Pigeon Forge, Tennessee, in summer 2027, bringing a major toy IP into the location-based entertainment space. Pigeon Forge is one of the most visited family tourism corridors in the US, making this a strategically significant opening. The move reflects the continued trend of major consumer brands investing in physical FEC experiences to drive engagement.

articles.title: Fun Spot America Announces Permanent Closure of Atlanta Location
articles.summary: Fun Spot America will permanently close its Atlanta-area park on August 2, 2026, ending operations at the Fayetteville, Georgia, location. CEO John Arie Jr. described the decision as extremely difficult, and the closure underscores ongoing viability challenges for standalone amusement parks in competitive markets. FEC operators and investors should note this as a data point on market saturation and cost pressures in the attractions space.

articles.title: Wavegarden Analysis: Surf Lagoons Generating Up to 9x Equity Multiples and 10x Land Value Gains
articles.summary: A new analysis from Wavegarden shows surf lagoon projects are delivering IRRs as high as 178% and land-value increases up to 10x across the US and internationally. These figures position surf attractions as a high-return anchor for mixed-use entertainment and real estate development. FEC developers and investors exploring large-format outdoor entertainment additions should pay close attention to this financial data.

articles.title: PICO and STYLY Expand LBE VR Content Distribution Partnership Globally
articles.summary: PICO's 4 Ultra Enterprise headsets have been selected as the hardware backbone for STYLY's growing location-based VR experience portfolio, covering nearly all its LBE projects worldwide. STYLY is evolving into a global LBE content distribution platform, and the PICO hardware was chosen for its ease of use with non-technical staff and diverse visitor demographics including young children and seniors. This partnership is relevant to FEC operators evaluating scalable, staff-light VR attraction solutions.

articles.title: Fort Worth's Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center to Close After Three Decades
articles.summary: Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center in Fort Worth, Texas, is shutting down after roughly 30 years in operation. The closure of a long-established, family-owned FEC highlights the challenges independent operators face in today's competitive entertainment landscape. This story is a reminder of the importance of reinvestment and modernization strategies for legacy FEC businesses.

articles.title: Three-Decade-Old Family-Owned FEC in Fort Worth Prepares to Shut Down
articles.summary: The Fort Worth Star-Telegram reports on the impending closure of Tornado Terry's, a family-owned amusement center that has operated for approximately 30 years. The story provides additional context on the business pressures that led to the decision, offering insights relevant to independent FEC operators navigating similar challenges. Operators should consider it alongside broader trends in independent venue viability.

articles.title: Star Factory Lantern Promotes Day-to-Night Dinosaur Strategy to Extend Guest Dwell Time
articles.summary: Star Factory Lantern is pitching a combined daytime animatronic and nighttime illuminated dinosaur experience designed to increase guest dwell time, secondary spending, and family engagement at parks and FECs. The concept offers operators a dual-revenue-window attraction format that can be layered onto existing footprints. This is worth evaluating for outdoor FEC operators and seasonal venues looking to maximize per-visit revenue.

articles.title: Slick City Action Park Opens at Potomac Mills Shopping Center in Northern Virginia
articles.summary: Slick City Action Park, an indoor slide and active entertainment concept, has opened a new location at Potomac Mills, one of the largest outlet malls in the US. The opening continues the trend of experiential FEC-style venues anchoring large retail centers as landlords seek traffic-driving tenants. Operators and developers should note the retail-entertainment co-location strategy as a site selection model.

articles.title: Kokomo Joe's FEC Adding Multi-Level Go-Kart Track
articles.summary: Kokomo Joe's is investing in a multi-level go-kart attraction, a capital upgrade that reflects the growing operator preference for elevated, differentiated kart experiences over standard single-level tracks. Multi-level tracks command higher price points and generate stronger social media visibility, making them a compelling ROI play for mid-size FECs. This is a useful benchmark for operators weighing go-kart reinvestment decisions.

articles.title: Chance Rides Emphasizes Long-Term Service and Parts Support as Key to Maximizing Ride ROI
articles.summary: Ride manufacturer Chance Rides is highlighting the importance of post-installation support — including parts availability, maintenance, and technical assistance — as a critical driver of long-term return on investment for operators. The company argues that the true measure of a ride's success is determined over years and decades, not just at opening. FEC operators and park managers should factor vendor service infrastructure into ride procurement decisions.

articles.title: Big Air Indoor Trampoline Park Opens New Location in Glendale, Arizona
articles.summary: Big Air, a national indoor trampoline park chain, is opening a new location in Glendale, Arizona, expanding its footprint in the competitive Southwest market. The opening is relevant for regional FEC operators tracking trampoline park growth and competitive density in the Phoenix metro area. It also signals continued franchise expansion activity in the indoor active entertainment segment.

articles.title: Arizona Business Publication Ranks Top 10 Indoor Entertainment Attractions for 2026
articles.summary: AZ Big Media has published its annual ranking of top indoor entertainment attractions in Arizona for 2026, providing a consumer-facing benchmark of competitive venues in one of the country's fastest-growing states. Rankings like this influence guest decision-making and can affect traffic at FECs both on and off the list. Arizona-based operators should review the list and consider how their venues compare on experience, marketing, and visibility.

articles.title: Lululand Adventure Park to Make Pennsylvania Debut in 2027
articles.summary: Lululand Adventure Park has announced plans to open its first Pennsylvania location next year, adding a new multi-attraction FEC player to the Mid-Atlantic market. The expansion is relevant for regional operators who will face increased competition and for suppliers seeking new venue clients. Details on attractions mix and location were not fully specified in early reports.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Releases 29-Country Research on Parents and Birthday Party Guest Lists
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has published new consumer research spanning 29 countries examining how parents approach birthday party guest list decisions. The data is valuable for FEC operators whose party packages are a core revenue driver, offering insight into consumer sentiment and planning behavior around group celebrations. Understanding these trends can help operators tailor party packages, pricing tiers, and group size accommodations.

articles.title: London-Originated Indoor Entertainment Venue with Virtual Soccer and Interactive Games Opens in Paris
articles.summary: A new indoor entertainment venue offering virtual soccer, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food has opened in Paris, expanding a London-based concept internationally. The venue format blends competitive socializing, food and beverage, and technology-driven attractions — a model gaining traction globally. FEC operators and developers should monitor this hybrid competitive-dining entertainment format as it crosses into new markets.

articles.title: ProFun Academy Partners with Ellis & Associates on Aquatic Safety Training for Attractions
articles.summary: ProFun Academy in Saudi Arabia has entered a strategic training partnership with Safety Skills Training DMCC, the international division of Ellis & Associates, to bring world-class aquatic safety and lifeguard training to attractions operators. For FEC and water park operators globally, access to internationally recognized safety training programs is a critical compliance and risk-management tool. This partnership expands the reach of E&A's safety standards into the Middle East market.

articles.title: Chalk & Steel Co. Plans New Indoor Entertainment Venue in Keller, Texas
articles.summary: Chalk & Steel Co. is bringing a new indoor entertainment concept to Keller, Texas, adding to the growing competitive landscape in the DFW metro area. The venue is part of a broader wave of new FEC openings in suburban Texas markets that are attracting investment due to population growth. Local operators should monitor this development as part of their competitive intelligence.

articles.title: High Tide VR Attraction Near Bethany Beach Offers Immersive LBE Experiences
articles.summary: High Tide VR, located near Bethany Beach, Delaware, is profiled as a distinctive entertainment option in a leisure-travel market typically dominated by outdoor activities. The venue illustrates how small-format VR attractions can carve out a niche in tourist destinations where guests seek weather-independent entertainment. FEC operators in coastal or resort markets may find the positioning strategy instructive.

articles.title: Splish Splash Water Park Hosts Private Early-Access Morning for Autism Society Members
articles.summary: Long Island's Splish Splash is offering Nassau/Suffolk Autism Society members and families two hours of low-crowd, low-stress park access before general public arrival on June 29. Inclusive programming like this builds community goodwill, drives group bookings, and addresses a growing demand for sensory-friendly entertainment options. FEC operators should consider similar programs as part of their community engagement and group sales strategies.

articles.title: New Adventure Park Billed as Unlike Any Other in the Country Opens in Atlanta
articles.summary: A new adventure park with a differentiated attractions mix has opened in Atlanta, entering a market just affected by Fun Spot America's announced closure. The timing highlights the dynamic churn of the FEC market in major metro areas where one closure can quickly be offset by a new entrant. Details on the specific attractions offered were limited in early reporting.

articles.title: UK Heatwave Drives Consumers Toward Indoor Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: Unusually high summer temperatures across the UK are prompting families to seek out indoor entertainment as a cool-weather alternative to outdoor activities. This trend creates a meaningful short-term demand spike for FEC operators with climate-controlled facilities in affected regions. UK-based FEC operators should consider timely marketing campaigns capitalizing on heat-driven footfall.

articles.title: New Indoor Entertainment Facility Set to Open in Springfield, Illinois in July
articles.summary: A new entertainment facility is scheduled to open in Springfield, Illinois, this July, adding a new FEC-style venue to the Central Illinois market. While details on the attraction mix remain sparse in early reports, the opening is relevant for Midwest FEC operators and suppliers tracking new market entrants. Local competitors should monitor the launch for competitive intelligence.

articles.title: Masti Zone Opens New Family Entertainment Destination in Greater Noida, India
articles.summary: Masti Zone has opened a new family entertainment destination at PKS Town Central in Greater Noida West, India, reflecting the rapid growth of the FEC format in South Asian suburban retail and mixed-use developments. The opening is relevant for suppliers and investors tracking international FEC market expansion beyond North America and Europe. It also highlights the retail-anchored FEC model gaining ground in emerging markets.
